summary: Completed backend testing of /api/euromillions/master-predictor endpoint with new Jack Patterns integration. All 28 tests pass. The 8 new Jack patterns (P1 Counting, Neighborhood Hunger, 49→45 Call, Quarter Echo, P4 Sequence, P1+P2 Digit Root, 8-Family Tracker, Circle Encoding) are properly integrated and 7/8 pattern emojis appear in generated tickets.

minor.issue: 🎭 Circle Encoding Missing pattern only triggers conditionally (when 48 without 47, or 50 without 49 in last draw) - this is correct behavior, not a bug

critical_code_review_comments: jack_patterns.py module is well-structured with clear documentation and helper functions
All 8 Jack patterns are properly integrated in euromillions_routes.py (lines 1775-1911)
Each pattern is wrapped in try/except for safety - good defensive coding
Pattern emojis verified: 🎻 P1 Count, 🍽️ Hungry, 🎵 Musical/49→45, 🔄 Q-Echo, 📊 P4 Seq, ∑ P1+P2, 8️⃣ Family all appear in output
🎭 Circle Encoding only triggers conditionally (correct behavior)
test_credentials: None required - all endpoints publicly accessible
seed_data_creation: N/A - using existing EuroMillions data from static Python files (MOCKED)
context_for_next_testing_agent: The EuroMillions master-predictor endpoint now includes 8 new 'Jack Patterns' discovered through user conversation. These patterns use emoji indicators: 🎻 P1 Count, 🍽️ Hungry, 🎵 49→45 Call/Musical, 🔄 Q-Echo, 📊 P4 Seq, 8️⃣ Family, 🎭 Missing encode, ∑ P1+P2 sum. All patterns are working correctly. The jack_patterns.py module imports correctly and all helper functions (circle, reverse_num) work as expected. EuroMillions data comes from static Python files, not live API (MOCKED).

mocked_apis.euromillions.api.pedromealha.dev: Data is cached in static Python files (euromillions_data_*.py)

patterns_found_in_output: 🎭 Circle Encoding Missing - CONDITIONAL (only triggers when 48 without 47, or 50 without 49)
